steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The first sentence establishes Keller’s vision: a communal indoor space that encouraged strolling and lingering. The second sentence describes his actual design — promenades, seating, fountains, greenery. The design is a direct result of the vision, so the transition must signal logical consequence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Regardless,": Signals that something happens despite an opposing force or obstacle. No obstacle is mentioned, so this transition misrepresents the relationship between vision and design. ✗
  • "By contrast,": Signals opposition or difference. The design does not contrast with the vision — it expresses it — so this transition is logically backwards. ✗
  • "Accordingly,": Signals that what follows is a logical consequence of what preceded it. The pedestrian-friendly promenades and seating follow logically from Keller’s vision of a communal strolling space. ✓
  • "In addition,": Signals an additional, parallel point. The design is not a separate new fact alongside the vision — it is the vision put into practice — so an additive transition misstates the relationship. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
